reviews.review_title: PROMISING FUN FACTOR - DEVS PLZ TOGGLE!
reviews.review_description:
So I MAY refund this, because I really dislike games not having a toggle option for holding weapons. You should at least be able to toggle grip the guns, this would be a deal breaker for me, but this game will go right back on Wishlist. That and the progression seems a little imbalanced and spread apart, I think meta progression and run upgrades like "replenish health" should be separate. 

There is no music which I find MOSTLY good for the mood but some more music might help here and there. 

I think if there was like a +XP indicator that pops up with hostages it would help and make their lack of animation and lip movement less awkward. 

The shotgun in particular doesn't work well with my gunstock (skol) and gunstocks would be wonderful with this game. Love the blood effect hitting the walls and floors, wish the stains didn't fade away. 

Fun factor overall is HUGE. I do think I'll need to revisit this game next update/sale in hope they add a toggle function for the weapon grip. I really loved clearing floors and leveling up though, if you don't think lack of toggle would bother you. PLAY! 

Hope it goes on sale again!
The game is a great concept but when 1 adjusts for height the gun mechanics are weird as well as gun shooting and hand placement...also be able to reset the stats.
best pve shooter ive ever played but i wish there was multiplayer

If your wondering if you should get this game... GET IT!!!! Its super fun and it doesn't get boring. I think for a early access it better then some non-early access games. I hope the developer sees this and sees other great reviews about this game. I love the way the blood works and how you go up the levels of the building and how it gets harder. If the developer sees this and responds, I was think to maybe reach out to some other early access creators like the one of Split Second for the violence or gun mechanics or Nightclub Simulator for some better blood, even though it's already AWESOME! Hope you reach out to some creator and keep on adding great updates. AMAZING GAME! Keep up the good work. And please respond. It would make my day.

1) Not left handed friendly. Can place weapon to the left hand but the ammo is placed on the left as well. 
2) No customization to place ammo or weapons on your person makes it difficult to tactically or even just quickly reload. I’ve come from Onward. This game has potential if it had the same dexterity as some of the better tuned FPS. 
3) overall weapon handling. I found myself grabbing a magazine when trying to load my shotgun or there would be odd times when my magazine had one bullet which must be a bug.
4) the repetitive handgun tutorial each time you start over is very annoying

I was really hoping for a fun new PvE shooter but this aint it.. yet.

This is an Indi game with a simple aesthetic but with good ideas. Not everything turns out as it should but it is “supportable-recommended.”
The game is simple… You're kind of like John McLain from Die Hard and you have/can clean your way up to the 50th floor if you can...
What good things does the game have:
-let you play the level from wherever you want so as not to get frustrated with repeating from below.
-You unlock weapons with experience.
-You unlock weapon upgrades such as sights or silencers with deaths
-Lets give orders to enemies to surrender…. But these have their own personality... some give up, others hesitate or threaten you... and others shoot you. And if they see you shooting at someone else and you get distracted, they lower their hands and either attack you or they lower their hands in disguise... that's fine.
You can put handcuffs to stop them.
-there are prisoners to be released 
-you can punch and make noise 
-scream to stop... if there are others nearby, they will hear it...
-There are options to crouch or stretch to look over places with the jostick.

Bad things to improve:
-basic aesthetics
-some regulatory menu such as not allowing you to choose how many degrees of rotation to snap, the only option is to move the camera a little before taking the snap... it hasn't made me dizzy but it is immediately different than usual.
-Between levels in the elevator it fails to indicate your experience, how many points per weapon for the next unlock... it only shows how much the total is to get but without knowing the information how far you are going.
If you mistakenly close the bonus menu for choosing new weapons/vest/medicine... The one for your 3 inventory slots appears but it leaves no option to see that previous menu again... (elevator area between levels).
-Some failure when lowering the enemy's arms to put the handcuff on. They get stuck... Since there is no tutorial, it is not known if you can use it with just one hand or you have to let go of the weapon... which is what I end up doing the most with the risk of the enemy resisting.
-Passing the level gives the same points by killing as by stopping enemies... it should be a bonus for stopping, being extra if it is more. If it works well, it penalizes killing stopped enemies.
-Weapons and things to hold on the body are not entirely comfortable. It would be nice to be able to customize the height and area of ​​the 3 weapon and ammunition slots.
-Aiming of the two-handed weapon is more displaced to the right than the real main hand (in my case right). I have to tilt my head to the right to aim centered.. 

Conclusion… I loved the concept, I liked unlocking weapons which makes it challenging to replay it.

The height in the game "always has to be updated by ringing the bell on the counter"... it would be good if by default the game loaded the game to do so after two seconds and thus not have to always do that. 
I trust they will make updates that improve these frictions, but at least it is enjoyable and recommended.

I hope they add haptic support from bhaptics and provolver to feel everything even more.

I need to let you know I only  bought this game to get a feel for Viper vice  Which is being made by the same developer as hardline. But this game is really fun level wave shooter, not perfect but really fun and for me that's what I look for in a game. There are some really cool features in this game,  you can talk to the npc and there pretty fun to hit , it would be nice if when you hit them in the stomach or Crotch they would  been over and react, but it's a pretty good game

reviews.review_title: Far from perfect, but incredibly fun
reviews.review_description: On one hand this is rather janky and clearly very low budget FPS, but on the other it really is a LOT of fun to play. The shooting is solid and the random floors and enemy placements (I just had three hiding in the same toilet together) mean there is a lot of replay value. In fact, it's so much fun to play, I'm willing to forgive its flaws - with one exception. The upgrade system, as it stands, is pretty worthless. Basically, you earn XP and every time you earn enough to reach the next level you get to choose an upgrade. However, the upgrade is one of three (seemingly random) items. Sometimes 2 of 3 will be different handguns, and there are no stats, so there's no way of knowing if it's worth the upgrade. The biggest issue is that after level 3 it takes so much XP to reach a new level that you could forget about the upgrades. What I would really like to see instead is a store between levels where you can spend XP. All items should be available (including health, though perhaps it should be expensive?) and have stats to let you know which upgrades are more worthwhile. Wipe the XP on death (or remove some - maybe have an option?) for a nice risk/reward incentive. You may disagree, but I think if that was implemented well it would be a lot more fun than spending ages to get to the next upgrade and there being nothing you want. Still an awesome game, but something for the developer to consider.
I luv this game please give it some color and more music too quiet ask me and ￼ and customization on guns like skins but so far great game
This has been great fun to play, with awesome potential! Looking forward to updates!!

reviews.review_title: It’s a great game to buy for now, but it
reviews.review_description: ￼ The game is great. It just needs to be a little bit more mechanic wise sharper away during the moving the character because sometime you grab the wrong stuff from your belt and then the gun starts glitching or hand. Also the medic could be like around the rooms when you’re going up because it doesn’t give you enough, if you start dying other than that, it’s a great game very fun to play minor glitches ￼
We need multiplayer RN
This game is so much fun but it feels weird to hold the two handed guns it doesn’t feel too bad but it doesn’t feel good but other than that this game is pretty good

This game has got a lot of potential. 
First, the graphics are decent even though the floors of the skyscraper look a bit loveless and empty. Enemies look good as does your handgun and your full body avatar. 
The angle with which you hold your gun feels a bit off and you have to twist your wrist a bit in order to shoot straight - maybe this could be changed a bit and if you want to hold the weapon with both hands you end up ejecting your magazine many times.

Now comes a part that this game makes way better than most shooters: You can threaten opponents and even knock them down so they become unconscious for some time which is useful when you run out of ammo and need to buy some time for a reload. What a great idea that is! It would be even better if you could grab your opponents weapons and use them against them.
Another great idea is that enemies have to reload their weapons as well which gives you time here and there 
A.I. could be a bit more realistic as opponents are just standing around waiting for you. Sometimes AI still  starts shooting through walls without seeing you.
Now you can also keep your weapons and choose the weapons you want to carry once they are upgraded. Great improvement here! Maybe a limited ammount of magazines would add a bit of realism and a knife as a deadly close combat weapon for silent removal could be added. 

Overall this game has some great ideas and if they could be imported to games like target react force or tactical assault which have better levels like small houses or more realistic cqc training houses but lack hand to hand options or non lethal options like threatening your enemies or grabbing and punching them, this game would get 5 star ratings easily.
Maybe there is a discord channel to further support the development.

Update: The dev really works on the game and it gets better and better with each update. Latest updates fixed some issues. Bullets fly a lot faster now and enemies seem to act more intelligently. Actually there is no need to see bullets flying as they are still too slow and this ruins gun handling a bit because you end up looking at the tracers and not your sights. 
Now you can command your enemies to surrender which is a great idea. Cuffing opponents is a bit difficult and many times you end up grabbing your own gear instead of the opponent's arms. Maybe opponents could just be cuffed by touching one wrist with the cuffs and you are done. What a great idea the taser is if you want to resort to non lethal violence. 
Gun handling still feels a bit strange sometimes but it's getting better! 

Again: It's great that the dev works on this game as I think it has got great potential and hopefully it will get further updates!

I like this game for a lot of reasons this reminds me of ready or not I want to say some suggestions 1 this game will be very good with blood splatters and blood pools 2 there should be a screen in the elevator that shows you what pistol you want to start off with 3 coop or ai teammates would be cool 4 also you should also start with a knife to 5 make the player look like swat member 6 more guns 7 breakable item like the windows and other stuff 8 limited amount of ammunition for the mk18 9 when you go behind a enemy and you press a button you can tell the nps to put it’s hands up and gun down those were all my suggestions for this game I hope the developer or developers put hard word in this game

developer_response.body:
Thanks for trying out my game!
Check out the latest build (v.0.3.1) you can now command the enemy to put up their hands, by pressing Y or B on your controller.

reviews.review_description:
There was a game back in the day that was glitchy as can be. It was called goat simulator. It was a hidden gem and a fun game. What gave it such charm was the glitches. This game in its current state reminds me of that. Not because it's anything like goat simulator but because the glitches Crack me up and I think this game is a lot of fun. It has that same charm to it. In it's current state It needs some work I think... But it is one of my favorite VR titles and I keep playing it. Over and over. It's fun. A lot of fun.

Here's what needs work. The gunplay is off. I have the red dot sight upgrade on the assault rifle and putting the dot on the enemy's head and pulling the trigger lands the bullet about 2 feet to the left of the dude I'm shooting at.

I get shot through walls, enemies get stuck in the walls/doors.

The XP system is frustrating. If I die my XP counter shouldn't go to 0 as that defeats the purpose of the rouge lite system. It takes a LONG time to level up. I have to clear 30 to 40 floors to get next level so starting at 0 xp is frustrating. I don't mind starting back on 1st floor, but the XP counter shouldn't be reset also.

The floors are bare. It's like the building is mostly cleared out. And none of the objects are interactive. If the computers, monitors, furniture could take damage or be picked up (thrown) and the windows could break I think that would propel this game way up in the immersion category. Being able to fall out the window, throw enemies out the window, throw a chair at an enemy when I need to reload. Smack a dude with a keyboard... So much I wish I could do here.

Bullet time. The game NEEDS bullet time. Remember Max Payne? The bullet time machanic in that game is exactly what this game is missing. Imagine leaping out of the elevator in slow motion blasting with a pistol in each of your big hands... That's what this game screams for. Which brings up this next question...

Why are my hands so big? Love that the game offers a full body avatar but my hands are like a bunch of bananas. Don't change this BTW... It's awesome and part of the games charm but man, the way he holds the gun and especially the rifle when you grab the front part to stabilize it... It's like Shaquille O'Neil holding a squirt gun. Big giant hands. In proportion to the wrist... I wonder does anyone else notice this? I like to get them to put they're hands up then I slap em... Right in the face. BAM! then I shoot em a lot. The Ole slap & shoot.

Sprinting is off. You have to really force the guy to sprint and it's hit or miss if that will work or not. I think implementing swing arm running would be beneficial in this game. The voice over, especially for the main character are hilarious. Shouting "don't move" ... Then they put up hands... you blast em "that's what you get"... Just like the cops in real life. 

So I'm giving the game 4 stats. Because I've had so much fun playing it. Despite the glitches and bugs I love this game as it is. I'd like to see some improvements. And bullet time would get the 5th star. But if you like shooting games this one's a good time.

UPDATE: The 1st paragraph of my review is no longer valid, a lot of the glitches have been worked out. Dev is super cool and takes constructive criticism like a professional and uses it to make his game better. Gone are the bare floors and empty rooms. The update is amazing cosmeticly. (the open walls on the construction level is sick! I LOVE throwing a dude off the side of the building *high five dev*) the shooting through doors/walls issue has actually gotten worse after the update but I'm sure that will change. I like the glass walls on some floors but they should shatter when you shoot them so that seems a misstep but hope to see that in a future update. Would be great if there were a few more guns to choose from, I think your grenades should respawn on your load out when you die. It takes so much XP to level up and a one time use grenade is not worth the time it takes to earn it. A Lazer sight for pistols would be a great attachment. The AI is above average at this point and I have no complaints on that. I still stand firm on the XP not resetting when you die, and the shooting accuracy still needs tweeked a bit. Bullet time/slow motion would still get my 5th star and I would love to give this title 5 stars. It's still one of my favorite VR games and I still love playing it. Hope it keeps getting updates and that it gains more popularity so the Dev can make some good cash, he deserves the good fortune. Check this game out, it's a great shooter.

developer_response.body:
Hi,
Hope you're doing well? :)
Check out the latest build (v.0.8.1).

I've added in weapon attachments and made sure the bad guys don't fire through walls anymore (hopefully).
I've also adjusted the XP system. You now only lose 25% of it. The reason I don't remove this feature completely, is because it's part of the challenge.

First of all, I would like to talk about the creator. I had an issue with the game (on meta’s behalf). The game uninstalled itself from my account and tried to make me re-buy it, I messaged the owner on TikTok and he responded with great help, but when it didn’t work he supplied me with a new key. He is so nice and understanding, consistent updates and just overall the reason I play my Vr. My TikTok is @Vr_Santa so go follow!!
